Level 1: I clearly name an app or a tool but it doesn’t appear to use AI.
Level 2: I clearly name an AI tool.
Level 3: I clearly name an AI tool and I describe how the AI tool works.
Level 4: I clearly name an AI tool . I describe how the AI tool works and provide a metaphor or example that helps readers understand.

Level 1: I make one prediction about how the tool may impact society.
Level 2: I make two predictions about how the tool may impact society.
Level 3: I make three predictions about how the tool may impact society. Not all of them are justified with evidence.
Level 4: I make three predictions about how the tool may impact society and I use evidence to justify each prediction.

Level 1: I make one recommendation but it amplifies societal harm.
Level 2: I make one recommendation but it is hard to tell how it might reduce societal harm.
Level 3: I clearly make one recommendation on how to regulate the tool in order to reduce societal harm.
Level 4: I clearly make one recommendation on how to regulate the tool in order to reduce societal harm. I also describe how others can help in getting these efforts started.

Level 1: The article’s main argument is unclear or the points included are unrelated to the argument made.
Level 2: The article contains each of the following structural components: argument, point 1, and conclusion.
Level 3: The article contains each of the following structural components: clear argument, point 1, point 2, point 3, and conclusion.
Level 4: The article contains each of the following structural components: hook, clear argument, point 1, point 2, point 3, “To Be Sure”, and a “Call to Action” conclusion.
